g.text: DRE 3D â€” LangGraph Deployment on Databricks
Where it runs Â· What hosts it Â· How it connects
Databricks Apps (Serverless Container) â†� WHERE LANGGRAPH RUNS
Databricks Services called by LangGraph tool nodes
from graph import app as lg_app
â€¢ Port 443 HTTPS (SSO via Databricks OAuth)
â€¢ Auto-scales 0 â†’ N replicas on demand
â€¢ Zero idle cost (scales to 0 when not used)
â€¢ Service identity: App service principal
â€¢ Runs entirely IN the container process
â€¢ NOT a separate service â€” just Python code
â€¢ State = Python TypedDict (in RAM per turn)
â€¢ Thread-safe: each session_id = one thread
â€¢ Served by Streamlit on same process
â€¢ Responses stream back via st.write_stream()
â€¢ Approval dashboard reads Delta directly
Cost cap: $X per domain per day
Auth: App service principal (UC row-level security)
DQ Backend runs as a SEPARATE Databricks App
Same LangGraph pattern â€” different graph definition
Runs on serverless job cluster, not Databricks Apps
Circuit breaker + domain agent dispatch inside graph
HTTP POST (LLM calls via AI Gateway)
: LangGraph is a Python library â€” it has NO dedicated Databricks hosting service.
It runs INSIDE the Databricks Apps container for interactive flows,
and INSIDE Databricks Workflow job tasks for async D2/D3 flows.
